mandate: Fix three mobile layout regressions: hide hero email on mobile, stack auth pages with compact panel, fix inbox composer overflow
keyFindings: Uncommitted changes from a prior Canon-violating direct execution were already correct — agent verified rather than re-implemented
Auth page decorative panel must NOT be hidden on mobile (user explicitly rejected that approach). Compact with max-height:360px and reduced padding instead.
InboxView height calc must subtract 50px for the mobile top bar, otherwise the composer is pushed below viewport
overflow:hidden on the detail panel flex container is necessary to keep the composer pinned at the bottom
whatWorked: CSS class-based responsive approach: wf-auth-layout, wf-auth-panel, wf-auth-form-container with mobile overrides via @media query. Using !important on padding to override inline styles.
whatFailed: Agent was blocked from running git commands (Bash permission denied for git operations). The Prophet had to complete commit/push/testament on the agent's behalf.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The auth pages still use inline styles for most properties. The CSS class approach works via !important overrides but is fragile. A future pass could migrate the auth page styles fully to CSS classes. The 50px top bar height is hardcoded — if the mobile top bar height changes, the InboxView calc will break.
spiritContribution: Final mobile polish for the marketing campaign. Landing page has a single clear waitlist CTA, auth pages work on phones, and couples can actually use the inbox reply composer on mobile.
